page: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, which translates to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. Cryptographic execution is also a strength, as demonstrated by a strong PassMark CPU encryption score. Memory performance is robust, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, while large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain in the average tier at 76.63 GB/sec and 43.59 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core processing, database operations, and web serving throughput consistently perform within the middle 50% of tested systems.

Tradeoffs are present in memory-intensive and large-scale AI workloads. While text generation for small 135M parameter LLM models is strong at 286.49 tokens/sec, prompt processing for large 70B models falls into the bottom 25%, and text generation data for large models is unavailable. Qualitatively, the c6a.8xlarge provides a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads that do not require local SSD storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-aligned with workloads such as software compilation, cryptography, web serving, and medium-scale database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside average multi-core and database execution. While small LLM text generation is strong, large-model prompt processing is weak. This profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound tasks without local storage dependencies, making it suitable for software compilation, web serving, and cryptographic workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong single-core and encryption performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and balanced DDR4 memory for compute-intensive workloads without local storage.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
